title: Maximum Reverse Palindromic Substring with K Swaps and Replacements

statement.markdown:
Given a string `s` and two integers `k` and `r`, find the maximum number of unique reverse palindromic substrings in `s` that can be obtained by replacing at most `r` characters and swapping at most `k` pairs of characters. A reverse palindromic substring is a substring that reads the same backwards as forwards. For example, 'abba' is a reverse palindromic substring of the string 'ababba'.
Example: Input: `s = 'abcdedcba', k = 2, r = 1`
Output: 3
Explanation: We can swap characters 'b' and 'd' once and replace character 'c' to form the maximum number of unique reverse palindromic substrings. In this case, we have the following substrings: 'abba', 'daba', 'edab'.
Example: Input: `s = 'abcdefg', k = 3, r = 2`
Output: 6
Explanation: We can replace all characters except the first one with any character and swap characters to get all unique reverse palindromic substrings in the string. In this case, we have the following substrings: 'a', 'a', 'a', 'a', 'b', 'c'.
statement.inputFormat: A string `s` and two integers `k` and `r`
statement.outputFormat: A positive integer representing the maximum number of unique reverse palindromic substrings
statement.notes: You can assume that the string `s` has a length at most 10000 and the characters are all lowercase English letters.

examples.explanation: We can swap characters 'b' and 'd' once and replace character 'c' to form the maximum number of unique reverse palindromic substrings.
We can replace all characters except the first one with any character and swap characters to get all unique reverse palindromic substrings in the string.

hints.text: Use dynamic programming to build up a table of maximum unique reverse palindromic substrings for each prefix of the string.

editorial.title: Editorial Solution
editorial.intuition: Use dynamic programming to build up a table of maximum unique reverse palindromic substrings for each prefix of the string.
editorial.bruteForce: Try all possible swap and replacement operations to find the maximum number of unique reverse palindromic substrings.
editorial.betterSolution: Use dynamic programming to memoize the results of subproblems and avoid duplicate computation.
editorial.optimalSolution: Maintain a table of maximum unique reverse palindromic substrings for each prefix of the string and update it as we perform swap and replacement operations.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Verify that the algorithm produces the correct result for all possible inputs using a formal proof.
editorial.dryRun: Use a dry run to test the algorithm with sample inputs and verify its correctness.
commonMistakes: Failing to memoize the results of subproblems
Failing to update the table of maximum unique reverse palindromic substrings correctly

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int maxReversePalindromicSubstring(string s, int k, int r) { int max = 0; for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) { for (int j = i + 1; j <= s.length(); j++) { string substr = s.substr(i, j - i); int rev_substring = substr.length(); for (int p = 0; p < rev_substring / 2; p++) { if (rev_substring == s.length()) break; if (s.charAt(rev_substring - p - 1) != substr.charAt(p)) break; } max = max + 1; } } return max; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: class Solution { public int maxReversePalindromicSubstring(String s, int k, int r) { int max = 0; for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) { for (int j = i + 1; j <= s.length(); j++) { String substr = s.substring(i, j - i); int rev_substring = substr.length(); for (int p = 0; p < rev_substring / 2; p++) { if (rev_substring == s.length()) break; if (s.charAt(rev_substring - p - 1) != substr.charAt(p)) break; } max = max + 1; } } return max; } }
buggyStarterCode.javascript: const Solution = function() { var maxReversePalindromicSubstring = function(s, k, r) { let max = 0; for (let i = 0; i < s.length; i++) { for (let j = i + 1; j <= s.length; j++) { let substr = s.substring(i, j - i); let rev_substring = substr.length; for (let p = 0; p < rev_substring / 2; p++) { if (rev_substring == s.length) break; if (s.charAt(rev_substring - p - 1) != substr.charAt(p)) break; } max = max + 1; } } return max; }; return maxReversePalindromicSubstring; };
